title: Neurophenomenology of an Altered State of Consciousness: An fMRI Case Study.

assets.abstract: A research participant came to our lab with self-proclaimed, ecstatic, Kundalini meditative experiences. Using neurophenomenology and functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI), we were able to identify brain activation in the left prefrontal cortex [primarily in left Brodmann׳s areas (BAs) 46 and 10, but also extending into BAs 11, 47, and 45] associated with this experience. The Phenomenology of Consciousness Inventory provided evidence that this was a perceived altered state of consciousnes

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Bridge paper score=10.0: links Heat/Fire concept to mechanistic neuroscience
